IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: WORKING TOGETHER WITH LOW-INCOME FAMILIES TO BUILD AND SUSTAIN HEALTHY HOMES AND COMMUNITIES.
IRS990.MissionDesc: IMPROVING THE LIVING CONDITIONS OF LOW INCOME FAMILIES THROUGH HOUSING AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT IN THE SAN JOAQUIN VALLEY.
IRS990.Desc: Self-Help housing Assisted 93 families in completing the construction of their new homes. 113 other families were in the process of construction as of June 30, 2024.Home buyer Assistance Program 20 families received secondary mortgage financing to help them purchase homes.Rehabilitation 56 families received financing and assistance in the rehabilitation or reconstruction of their homes. Multifamily Housing 173 Rental Units Developed Guardian Village (48)Neighborhood Village (53)Northstar Apartments (72)On June 30, 2024, SHE owned and operated 2,641 units of rental housing in 55 affordable housing communities. Community Development: 480 water/sewer connections in small rural communitiesHomeownership Education and Counseling: 718 low-income households received individual and/or classroom homeownership training to prepare them for home ownership. Emergency Services: For FY 24, Emergency ServicesInstalled 47 Emergency Water Tanks.Delivered Hauled water to around 317 households.Completed new construction or repairs on 58 new wells.Tested 86 domestic wells for contamination.Installed 34 filtration devices to mitigate contamination in systems.Delivered drinking water to around 3,317 households.

BusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: William Philips dba Aero Pure Water

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Self-Help Enterprises is a tax-exempt corporation under section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code and section 23701(d) of the State of California Corporations Code. The Organization is subject to taxation on any unrelated business income.The Organizations wholly-owned subsidiaries were organized as either a tax-exempt corporation, partnership, limited liability company or, in two cases, as a taxable corporation. The accompanying consolidated financial statements do not include a provision for federal or state income tax expense or benefit arising from net income or loss reported in the accompanying consolidated statements of activities attributable to these wholly-owned subsidiaries. In accordance with the generally accepted method of presenting partnership and limited liability company financial statements in the United States of America, such income or loss is includable in the taxable income or loss of the respective partner(s)/members(s). Limited liability companies are subject to a California gross receipts fee and limited partnerships are subject to a California minimum franchise fee of $800.Generally accepted accounting principles provides accounting and disclosures guidance about positions taken by an entity in its tax returns that might be uncertain. Management has considered its tax positions and believes that all of the positions taken in its federal and state exempt organization and other tax returns are more likely than not to be sustained upon examination. The Organizations returns are subject to examination by federal and state taxing authorities, generally for three years and four years, respectively, after they are filed.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FORM 990 IS PRESENTED TO THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS FOR REVIEW.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ANNUAL REVIEW OF THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AND DISCLOSURE BY BOARD MEMBERS AND OFFICERS. ANY IDENTIFIED CONFLICT OF INTEREST IS REVIEWED BY THE CEO FOR MITIGATION OR ACTION. IF THE CONFLICT IS WITH THE CEO IT IS REVIEWED BY THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD FOR MITIGATION OR ACTION.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 15a
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE PRESIDENT/CEO COMPENSATION PACKAGE IS REVIEWED BY THE BOARD ON AN ANNUAL BASIS USING DATA FROM COMPARABLE PEER ORGANIZATIONS. OTHER OFFICERS ARE PAID IN ACCORDANCE WITH ESTABLISHED COMPANY WIDE COMPENSATION RANGES APPROVED BY THE BOARD. ALL RANGES ARE DETERMINED BASED ON A COMBINATION OF INFORMATION FROM FORM 990'S AVAILABLE IN GUIDESTAR, TARGETED COMPENSATION SURVEYS AND INTERNAL ASSESSMENT OF RETENTION AND HIRING EXPERIENCE. THE RANGES MAY BE ADJUSTED BY COST OF LIVING ADJUSTMENT EACH YEAR.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, Line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.
Organization did not change their process of reviewing the audited financial statements. An audit committee is appointed that assumes responsibility for oversight of the annual audit.
